name: claude-code-usage
description: Monitor Claude Code usage and costs in VSCode status bar
author: GrowthJack

claudeCodeUsage.dataDirectory.description: Custom Claude data directory path (leave empty for auto-detection)

claudeCodeUsage.decimalPlaces.description: Number of decimal places for cost display

claudeCodeUsage.compactNumbers.description: Show large token counts compactly (e.g. 1.2M, 345K) instead of full numbers.

claudeCodeUsage.timezone.description: IANA timezone for date display (e.g. "Asia/Hong_Kong", "UTC"). Leave empty to use the system timezone. Useful inside sandboxes/devcontainers. Day-boundary calculations still follow the system timezone.

claudeCodeUsage.showCost.description: Show today's cost in the status bar. Disable to hide the cost item — the dashboard still shows all cost figures.

claudeCodeUsage.showContext.description: Show the current session's context-window fill (like /context) in the status bar. Estimated from the latest log record, so it updates with each new message.

claudeCodeUsage.usageLimitTracking.description: Show real 5-hour and weekly limit utilisation in the status bar. Uses Claude Code's existing sign-in (~/.claude/.credentials.json or macOS Keychain) — no configuration needed.

claudeCodeUsage.workflowQuotaWarnPercent.description: Show a dismissible warning banner in the dashboard when the remaining 5-hour quota drops below this percentage — a guard against starting a workflow (ultracode) run that the window can't finish. Set 0 to disable.

claudeCodeUsage.advice.apiKey.description: API key for the 'Get AI Advice' feature (OpenAI-compatible endpoint, e.g. DeepSeek). Leave empty to disable.

claudeCodeUsage.advice.apiUrl.description: Chat-completions endpoint for the advice feature (OpenAI-compatible). For DeepSeek use https://api.deepseek.com/chat/completions (no /v1).

claudeCodeUsage.advice.model.description: Model name used by the advice feature.

claudeCodeUsage.advice.userContext.description: Optional background about yourself or the project (e.g. "non-programmer building a VS Code extension; expect heavy exploratory tool use"). When set, AI advice ends with a 'Personalised for this project' section calibrated against it.

claudeCodeUsage.advice.backend.description: Where AI advice (and the Usage Optimizer) send their request. 'subscription' reuses your existing Claude Code sign-in (no API key) and a cheap model — it uses a little of your Claude quota. 'api' uses the advice.apiKey / apiUrl / model below.

claudeCodeUsage.advice.apiFormat.description: Request format for the 'api' backend: 'anthropic' (the /v1/messages shape, the default) or 'openai' (the chat-completions shape, e.g. DeepSeek and other OpenAI-compatible proxies).

claudeCodeUsage.advice.subscriptionModel.description: Model used by the 'subscription' backend. A cheap model (Haiku) is recommended so advice barely touches your quota.

claudeCodeUsage.advice.optimizer.enabled.description: Show the Usage Optimizer on the Content tab. Off by default. When on, you can paste a rough request and get back a tightened, paste-ready prompt plus a recommended effort / thinking / model. Only the text you paste is sent to the configured advice backend — never to Claude Code's terminal. A one-time consent prompt appears on first use.

claudeCodeUsage.advice.promptWindowDays.description: How many days of your own prompts the AI advice feature samples when summarising your usage. Smaller = more recent habits; larger = a broader sample.

claudeCodeUsage.advice.reasoningEffort.description: Reasoning effort (DeepSeek V4 supports 'high' and 'max'). Leave empty to disable.

claudeCodeUsage.enableContentAnalysis.description: Run the content/token analysis on conversation logs and show the Content tab. Disable to skip the CPU-heavy text scan during refresh.

claudeCodeUsage.analysis.calibrate.description: Calibrate the Content tab's per-category token figures: keep the text-length estimate for relative shares, then scale them to the exact billed token totals (assistant categories to real output tokens; user/tool-result categories to real input + cache-write tokens). Improves absolute accuracy without changing within-side shares. Disable to show the raw text-length estimates.

claudeCodeUsage.projectGroupingMode.description: How the Projects tab groups working directories. 'git' groups by enclosing git repository (default); 'folder' groups by the heuristic top-level project folder only (no filesystem walk); 'flat' shows every working directory as its own row.

claudeCodeUsage.fileWatching.description: Watch Claude Code log files and refresh the status bar within ~1.5s of each new message. Disable to fall back to the slower interval-based refresh (claudeCodeUsage.refreshInterval) — useful if the constant refresh feels distracting during active sessions.

claudeCodeUsage.pauseDashboardRefresh.description: Skip the dashboard webview on automatic refreshes (status bar still updates). When enabled, the dashboard only updates when you run 'Refresh Usage Data' manually — handy if a constantly-reloading dashboard interferes with reading numbers while an agent is actively writing.
